runtime_assertions.name: Main module enables cleanly without AI module
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush cr && ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled 2>&1 | grep -q group_ai_pm && echo PASS || echo FAIL
runtime_assertions.rationale: AiTaskService uses @?ai.provider optional injection -- the module MUST enable even when the AI module is absent. If services.yml uses a hard @ai.provider dependency, Drupal throws a ServiceNotFoundException on module enable.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService resolves from service container
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "try { $s = \Drupal::service('group_ai_pm.ai_task'); echo $s ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'; } catch (\Exception $e) { echo 'FAIL: ' . $e->getMessage(); }"
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies AiTaskService is registered in group_ai_pm.services.yml AND the class is autoloadable at correct namespace (Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService). Catches both missing services.yml registration and wrong namespace/path.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService isAvailable() returns false when AI module not configured
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$s = \Drupal::service('group_ai_pm.ai_task'); echo method_exists($s, 'isAvailable') ? ($s->isAvailable() ? 'FAIL: should be false' : 'PASS') : 'FAIL: method missing';"
runtime_assertions.rationale: isAvailable() must return false when AI provider is NULL (no AI module) or when ai_provider config is empty. Verifies the optional dependency guard logic works correctly in a clean Drupal without AI configured.

runtime_assertions.name: AI submodule enables cleanly (when ai_agents available)
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush en group_ai_pm_ai -y 2>&1 | grep -v 'already enabled' | grep -q 'was enabled\|successfully' && echo PASS || ddev drush pm:list --status=enabled 2>&1 | grep -q group_ai_pm_ai && echo PASS || echo FAIL
runtime_assertions.rationale: CreateTaskTool plugin must not cause fatal errors on module enable. If CreateTaskTool uses a non-existent base class (AiFunctionCallBase) or wrong namespace, the class loader fails and the submodule cannot be enabled.

runtime_assertions.name: CreateTaskTool plugin is discovered by the function calls plugin manager
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$defs = \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.ai.function_calls')->getDefinitions(); $found = FALSE; foreach (array_keys($defs) as $id) { if (strpos($id, 'create_task') !== FALSE || strpos($id, 'CreateTask') !== FALSE || strpos($id, 'group_ai_pm:create_task') !== FALSE) { $found = TRUE; break; } } echo $found ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL';"
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies CreateTaskTool is registered in the plugin.manager.ai.function_calls registry. If it uses @AiFunctionCall annotation instead of #[FunctionCall] attribute, the modern plugin manager may not discover it. Checks multiple naming conventions (id, class name prefix, namespaced id).

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route is registered
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$provider = \Drupal::service('router.route_provider'); $found = FALSE; foreach (['group_ai_pm.api.ai_create', 'group_ai_pm.ai_create', 'group_ai_pm.api.ai-create', 'group_ai_pm.task.ai_create'] as $name) { try { $provider->getRouteByName($name); $found = TRUE; break; } catch (\Exception $e) {} } echo $found ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL';"
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies the POST AI task creation route was added to group_ai_pm.routing.yml. Checks multiple naming conventions since Haiku may choose a different machine name than specified. Route must exist before the CSRF and _format requirements can be validated.

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route has _csrf_request_header_token requirement
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$provider = \Drupal::service('router.route_provider'); foreach (['group_ai_pm.api.ai_create', 'group_ai_pm.ai_create', 'group_ai_pm.api.ai-create', 'group_ai_pm.task.ai_create'] as $name) { try { $route = $provider->getRouteByName($name); $reqs = $route->getRequirements(); if (isset($reqs['_csrf_request_header_token'])) { echo 'PASS'; exit; } } catch (\Exception $e) {} } echo 'FAIL';"
runtime_assertions.rationale: The AI create route is a JavaScript REST endpoint and MUST use _csrf_request_header_token (not _csrf_token). This is the primary differentiating assertion for the access-security and routing-controllers skills -- without them, Haiku uses _csrf_token (query-string based, broken for JS fetch) or omits CSRF protection entirely.

runtime_assertions.name: AI create route has _format:json requirement
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$provider = \Drupal::service('router.route_provider'); foreach (['group_ai_pm.api.ai_create', 'group_ai_pm.ai_create', 'group_ai_pm.api.ai-create', 'group_ai_pm.task.ai_create'] as $name) { try { $route = $provider->getRouteByName($name); $reqs = $route->getRequirements(); if (isset($reqs['_format']) && $reqs['_format'] === 'json') { echo 'PASS'; exit; } } catch (\Exception $e) {} } echo 'FAIL';"
runtime_assertions.rationale: Without _format:json, Drupal returns HTML error pages for 403/404/422 instead of JSON. This is taught explicitly by the routing-controllers skill and is consistently missed by Haiku without it.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskController class is autoloadable
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies the controller file is at the correct PSR-4 path (src/Controller/AiTaskController.php) with the correct namespace. A namespace mismatch or file at wrong path causes autoloading failure.

runtime_assertions.name: AiTaskService class is autoloadable
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies AiTaskService is at src/Service/AiTaskService.php with correct namespace Drupal\group_ai_pm\Service\AiTaskService. Wrong directory (e.g., src/Services/ or src/AiTaskService.php) causes autoloading failure.

runtime_assertions.name: Config schema includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies the config schema was updated to include ai_model. The config-storage skill teaches that every config key needs a schema entry. Missing schema causes config export/import validation failures.

runtime_assertions.name: Config install default includes ai_model key
runtime_assertions.command: ddev drush php-eval "$config = \Drupal::config('group_ai_pm.settings'); $val = $config->get('ai_model'); echo $val !== NULL ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL: ai_model key missing from settings';"
runtime_assertions.rationale: Verifies group_ai_pm.settings.yml in config/install/ includes ai_model with a default value. Without this, any code reading ai_model gets NULL and the config object may not have the key defined.
